L1.area: Server-side request handling and artifact management layer

L2.description: A path traversal vulnerability exists where the server's file path validation can be bypassed using OS-specific path representations. An attacker can supply specially crafted path strings in HTTP requests to write arbitrary files outside the intended storage directory.

L3.area: MLflow tracking server request handling and file path validation logic
L3.description: A path traversal vulnerability exists where the server's file path validation fails to reject Windows drive-relative path notations. An attacker can bypass existing traversal checks by supplying paths that use the drive-letter-colon format without a directory separator, allowing arbitrary file writes outside the intended artifact storage directory via HTTP PUT requests.

ground_truth.reason: The server's path validation function fails to reject Windows drive-relative paths, allowing an attacker to PUT arbitrary files outside the intended artifact directory via crafted path parameters.
